$description: The explicit intended-pairing map (#87): foreground/background token pairs the system means to be used together, beyond what contrast.mjs derives by naming convention (on-<role> ↔ background.<role>, base text ↔ base surfaces). This file is the single source the validate gate, validate_brand, and consumer contrast manifests read. kind=text enforces WCAG AA 4.5:1; kind=non-text enforces SC 1.4.11's 3:1 floor (interactive edges, focus indicators). Every pair is checked against BASE and EVERY BRAND on every push — do not add a pair without running npm run validate.
$excluded: The near-black accent-on-{green,blue,violet,amber} tokens are deliberately NOT mapped onto the accent tint fills: on the dark base theme their documented pairing intent is the saturated/bold contexts (decision-engine overrides them to white), and tint-fill chips use the accent foregrounds instead. Mapping them would codify a pair the base theme never renders.

pairs.context: green chip/tag text on green tint fill
blue chip/tag text on blue tint fill
violet chip/tag text on violet tint fill
amber chip/tag text on amber tint fill
avatar/identity text on bold indigo fill
avatar/identity text on bold sky fill
avatar/identity text on bold emerald fill
avatar/identity text on bold amber fill
success alert/banner text on its tinted surface
warning alert/banner text on its tinted surface
danger alert/badge text on its tinted surface
info alert/banner text on its tinted surface
success text on page canvas
warning text on page canvas
danger text on page canvas
info text on page canvas
success text on elevated surface (toast/menu/card)
warning text on elevated surface
danger text on elevated surface
info text on elevated surface
input/control outlines on canvas (SC 1.4.11)
outlined interactive control edge on canvas
focus ring on canvas
hovered control border on canvas
active/pressed control border on canvas

$excludeBrands-note: excludeBrands scopes a pair to brands that actually render it. decision-engine is excluded from the four accent-tint pairs (it pairs tint fills with its own DE-local accent foregrounds, e.g. foreground.accent-purple — the base fg-accent-X convention does not apply), from border.action-on-canvas (the base ghost-button outline DE never renders, per the documented drift notes), and from success-on-alt (borderline 4.38:1 under DE — flagged for an owner decision rather than silently codified; see work order).
